name: S&P 500
summary: 6,817 (-0.1% Apr 10) — snapped 7-day streak on CPI. Week +3.6%. VIX 19.23 = cheapest war insurance. Asymmetry NEGATIVE for optimistic positioning: Islamabad success mostly priced, failure = VIX 30+, S&P -3-5%.

price_snapshots.trigger: Largest single-session rally since May — Pezeshkian peace signal
price_snapshots.delta_context: 441 of 500 components green. VIX collapsed to 24.40 (below 30 threshold = algo selling stops). NASDAQ +3.83%, Dow +2.49%. SMH +5.8%. XLE -1.1% (explicit: bad for oil producers, good for economy). IRGC corporate threat deadline tonight = binary risk event.
Pre-ceasefire flat day; ceasefire-day relief rally pending Wed open

price_snapshots.trigger: Ceasefire rally — best day since April 2025
price_snapshots.delta_context: +2.51%; VIX crushed to 21; airlines exploded (Delta +11%, United +14%); defense +4.0% anomaly

signals.content: Energy and defense stocks surged: Lockheed +15%, Exxon +5%+, Northrop +6%. Investors aggressively rotating from tech/growth into defense and energy.
S&P 500 dropped 1.5%, Nasdaq 100 plunged 1.8% to six-month lows. Correction territory. Stocks, bonds, and gold all selling off simultaneously.

S&P 500 soared 2.2% (+143 pts) to 6,605 after Trump announced 5-day strike pause. Major relief rally. US markets reacted to de-escalation signal; Asian markets had already sold off on the earlier escalation threats (time-zone divergence).

S&P 500 at 6,369 (-7.8% monthly, -1.7% daily). VIX surged 13.2% to 31.05 — crossing 30 triggers institutional risk-off (algo selling, portfolio de-risking). Breakdown: ~55% crisis-driven (Houthi entry, Saudi base attack, Tel Aviv strike, regional bombardment), ~30% tariff-driven (Section 122 tariffs 10-15% global, Yale Budget Lab: +0.5-0.6% price level, +0.3pp unemployment), ~15% other. VIX above 30 creates self-reinforcing cycle: VIX up → algos sell equities → equities drop → VIX higher.

S&P 500 at 6,343.72 (-0.4%, -7.8% monthly). VIX dropped below 30 to 28.61 (-6.5%) on Trump 'willing to end war' headlines — technically significant as VIX >30 triggers institutional algo selling. SMH (semiconductors) -3.1% on TurboQuant + helium. Defense (ITA) -2.4% on peace headlines + Boeing NGAD + EU EDIP. KOSPI -4.3% is a leading indicator of semiconductor supply chain stress. Market pricing 52% probability of Fed HIKE by year-end (first time >50%). Gold regime shift, if confirmed, extends equity drawdown window. CONFIRMED.

S&P surged +2.91% to 6,528.52 — best day since May. Triggered by Iran's Pezeshkian telling EU he has 'necessary will' to end war. 441 of 500 components green. VIX collapsed to 24.40, firmly below 30 institutional threshold (algo selling stops = self-reinforcing equity bid). NASDAQ +3.83% (Nvidia +5%, Meta +6%), Dow +2.49%. SMH +5.8% on helium relief. XLE -1.1% while S&P +2.9% — explicit market signal: de-escalation good for consumers/economy, bad for producers. DXY dropped below 100 to 99.47. Brent crashed -14% to $101.76 — LARGEST decline since war began. HOWEVER: IRGC threatened 18 named US companies (Apple, Google, Nvidia, JPMorgan) with deadline tonight 20:00 Tehran — if executed, VIX spikes back above 30 triggering algo selling cascade. Rally built on REPORTED peace signals, not confirmed de-escalation — same pattern that produced March 23 crash reversal. (Sources: Yahoo Finance, CNBC, Bloomberg)

S&P gave back -0.52% to 6,541 after yesterday's +2.91% rally — partial profit-taking or April 6 deadline caution. Market explicitly discounted IRGC corporate threat credibility: university deadline (Mar 30) produced no attack, corporate deadline (Apr 1) produced single AWS hit in Bahrain, and market rallied +2.91% through the deadline. IRGC credibility discount now priced in. Pattern: each successive IRGC threat produces less market reaction.

VIX fell to 24.51 (-2.8%) — two consecutive sessions below 30 institutional threshold, reducing cascade selling risk. Algo selling stops when VIX below 30, creating self-reinforcing equity bid. BUT 69.1% 3-month increase from pre-crisis 14.50 shows structural fear remains elevated. April 6 energy strike deadline could spike VIX back above 30 in a single session, reactivating algo selling cascade.

Bond-equity divergence — equities celebrate de-escalation but 10Y yield FELL (-2bps to 4.30%), pricing growth deterioration and recession risk, not inflation relief. ISM Prices Index at 78.3 (highest since June 2022) but employment in contraction = textbook STAGFLATION signal. When bond and equity markets diverge on the same news, bonds are usually right. This divergence is the most important risk signal for equity positioning.

POST-ADDRESS: VIX surged +11.4% to 27.34 from 24.51 — approaching the 30 institutional threshold (only 2.66 points away). Trump 'stone ages' address restored war uncertainty, reversing VIX's two consecutive sessions below 30. US market interpretation DIVERGED from Asia/Europe: KOSPI -4.5%, DAX -2.3%, Nikkei -2.4% heard 'war escalation.' US selectively bought defense (ITA +2.2%, LMT +2.2%) and tech (SMH +2.2%, NASDAQ +1.2%) while selling energy (XLE -3.7%, Exxon -5.2%). The widest US/Asia interpretation divergence since war began. Bond market sided with Asia — 10Y at 4.32% barely moved. One of these interpretations is wrong. (Sources: Cboe, yfinance, multiple market data)

S&P 500 closed April 2 at 6,583 (+0.1%) — REMARKABLE recovery after losing $550B in 25 minutes during Trump's primetime address. Weekly +3.4%, best since late November, ending 5 consecutive losing weeks. But the OIL-EQUITY DIVERGENCE is the signal: WTI +11.9% same day. Oil pricing escalation, equities pricing 2-3 week end. This split resolves violently in the April 5-8 window. ISM Prices 78.3 (highest since June 2022). Market pricing 52% Fed hike probability by year-end — first time above 50%. (Sources: Fortune, Bloomberg, CNBC, Yahoo Finance)

S&P 500 at 6,583 (+0.1% April 3). Down 4% YTD. Oil-equity divergence at widest of war: WTI +11.9% while S&P +0.1%. S&P recovered from $550B intraday crash during Trump speech. April 4 range 6,475-6,602 (Good Friday, reduced liquidity). VIX at 23.87 pricing ZERO probability of Iran US base strike. If April 6 deadline expires + Iran retaliates, VIX past 30 = algo selling cascade. (Sources: yfinance, Fortune, Motley Fool)

S&P futures at 6,604 (-0.28% pre-market). VIX still at 23.87 — massively mispriced for Tuesday's binary event (April 6 deadline extension). Markets reopening Monday into the deadline extension window. Oil-equity divergence remains the widest of the war and unresolved. April 5-8 is the densest event cluster of the conflict: deadline extension, RBI MPC, TrendForce announcement window, helium production cut watch. VIX below 30 means algo selling cascade not active, but only 6 points from threshold. (Sources: yfinance, Cboe)

S&P 500 at 6,612 (+0.4%), four consecutive winning days on ceasefire HOPES despite ceasefire being REJECTED. VIX at 24.17 (+1.3%) — MASSIVELY MISPRICED for Tuesday deadline binary. Expected VIX 28-32 given deadline imminence + rejection. Market pricing 'they'll figure it out' consensus. VIX below 30 = institutional algo selling OFF = self-reinforcing equity bid. IRGC credibility discount suppressing vol. IF deadline passes without deal, VIX spike to 30+ triggers mechanical algo selling cascade amplifying equity decline 2-3x. $1.5T FY2027 defense budget creates inflationary impulse (government spending) compounding oil-driven inflation — defense stocks now 4.2% of S&P. Lockheed Martin +2.4% to $637.90. (Sources: yfinance, CNBC, FinancialContent)

VIX 24.17 / 24.54 STILL MASSIVELY MISPRICED FOR DEADLINE BINARY [CONFIRMED]. With deadline expiring TONIGHT at 8 PM ET, VIX should be 28-32 (significant binary event risk premium). Instead VIX closed 24.54 on April 6 and intraday 24.17 on April 7. 'Volatility thaw' narrative dominated April 6 wires (FinancialContent: 'Volatility Thaw: VIX Retreats to 24.54'). S&P 4th consecutive up day reinforced complacency. Option market making selling premium into 'deal hope' headlines. VIX 24.17 implies ~8-10% probability of a 5%+ S&P move in 10 days — actual probability given tonight's binary is 25-35%. Largest VIX-vs-event mispricing of the war. S&P closed +0.4% at 6,611.83 (4th up day). Futures 6,640 (+0.66%). Oil-equity divergence WIDENED by 1 standard deviation: Brent +20.6% 1M while S&P -3.2% 1M. Either oil is wrong (peace coming) or equities are wrong (escalation coming). Today is the day they reconcile. ANY VIX call with strike 30-35 and 7-14 DTE is asymmetric.

EVENING UPDATE: S&P 6,612 (+0.4%), VIX 25.63 (+6.0%) — VIX MASSIVELY MISPRICED. VIX implies ~8-10% probability of 5%+ S&P move in 10 days; actual probability 25-35% given tonight's binary. Expected VIX 28-32. Gap exists because: IRGC credibility discount, VIX below 30 = institutional algo selling OFF, 'buy stocks buy puts' positioning. Goldman 30% / Moody's 48.6% recession probability. March NFP +178K vs 60K expected — strongest non-war data. Q1 earnings season starts April 14 (JPM, BAC, UNH). Oil-equity divergence: Brent +20.6% 1M vs S&P -3.2% 1M — today they reconcile. (Sources: CBOE, Goldman Sachs, Moody's, Bloomberg)

S&P 500 TUESDAY CLOSE 6,616.85 (+0.08%) — FUTURES +2.4% POST-CEASEFIRE [CONFIRMED]. Tuesday cash close S&P 6,616.85 (+0.08%), NASDAQ 22,017.85 (+0.10%), Dow 46,584.46 (-0.18%). After hours, futures jumped on the ceasefire announcement: S&P futures +2.4%, Nasdaq 100 +3.0%, Dow +2.2% (~+1,000 points). Wednesday open will print sharply higher. Sector rotation expected: Energy (XLE) DOWN (yesterday's bull thesis reverses), Defense (ITA) DOWN (rotation unwind), Financials UP (relief + lower yields = better banks), Semiconductors (SMH) UP (riding global tech rally). Cheniere LNG likely down on TTF reversal. The risk-on cascade extends if no negative news during European session.

S&P 500 closed at 6,782.81 (+2.51%) on April 8 — best day since April 2025. NASDAQ +2.80% to 22,635. Dow +2.66% to 47,909. Airlines led: Delta +11%, United +14%, American +11%, Southwest +13%. Tech surged: SMH +5.8% on helium relief. Energy crashed: XLE -3.5%, Exxon -4.7%. Defense ANOMALY: ITA +4.0% ON ceasefire — $1.5T FY27 budget makes defense structural, not war-dependent. VIX crashed to 21.04 from 25.63 — below pre-war level. April 9: flat/consolidation. Rally FRAGILE: built on ceasefire that fractured within 18 hours. VIX at 21 means no protective hedging — reversal amplified 2-3x. Friday dual binary: CPI (3.2-3.4%) + Islamabad talks. (Source: CNBC, Yahoo Finance, Bloomberg, Investing.com)

S&P 500 closed at 6,816.89 (-0.10%), snapping 7-day winning streak [CONFIRMED, Regal Securities]. Dow -0.56%. Nasdaq +0.37% (8th consecutive day — Nvidia +2.57%, AMD +3.55%). Market initially surged on CPI relief (3.3% below feared 3.5%+) then reversed. VIX compressed further to 19.23 — vol-selling feedback loop continues [CONFIRMED, CBOE]. For the week: S&P +3.6%, Nasdaq +4.7%, Dow +3.0% — best week since November. VIX at 19.23 implies ~8-10% probability of 5%+ move in 10 days; actual probability 25-35% given Islamabad binary. This is the cheapest insurance since the war started.

edges.context: Primary US equity benchmark. Bond-equity divergence emerging — equities pricing de-escalation while bonds pricing recession. ISM stagflation signal (prices 78.3, employment contracting).
Rate-cut probability nearly doubled post-ceasefire (25% to 43%). VIX crushed to 21. Rally fragile — built on ceasefire that fractured within 18 hours.

Oil-equity divergence RESOLVED on ceasefire: Brent -11.5% and S&P +2.51% moved in expected directions. Energy XLE -3.5%, airlines +11-14%. If ceasefire collapses and oil snaps to $110+, S&P reversal amplified by VIX at 21.
SMH +5.8% on helium relief. Ceasefire bought time for fab allocations. No TrendForce production-cut announcement by Day 2 of window. By April 10, helium cliff bear case functionally dead.

trigger_points.condition: S&P 500 enters bear market (down 20% from peak)
trigger_points.mechanism: Would force Fed to consider emergency support despite inflation. Would crash global markets including India. Could paradoxically lead to war de-escalation pressure.
